text: Originally developed for natural language processing, the self-attention mechanism has recently achieved remarkable success across various computer vision domains. However, directly applying self-attention to images presents three significant hurdles: first, flattening images into 1D sequences overlooks their inherent 2D spatial structures; second, the quadratic computational complexity is prohibitive for high-resolution inputs; and third, standard self-attention primarily focuses on spatial adaptability while neglecting channel-wise dynamics. In this paper, we introduce Large Kernel Attention (LKA), a novel linear attention module designed to harness long-range correlations and self-adaptivity while circumventing these limitations. Building on this, we develop the Visual Attention Network (VAN), a straightforward yet powerful architecture. Despite its simplicity, VAN consistently matches or outperforms established convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs) across a wide range of tasks, including image classification, object detection, and various forms of segmentation. Notably, the VAN-B6 variant achieves 87.8% accuracy on ImageNet and sets a new state-of-the-art for panoptic segmentation with 58.2 PQ. Furthermore, VAN-B2 significantly outperforms Swin-T on the ADE20K semantic segmentation benchmark (50.1 vs. 46.1 mIoU) and the COCO object detection dataset (48.8 vs. 46.2 AP). Our work offers a fresh perspective on attention mechanisms and provides a robust, simple baseline for the vision community. Code and models are available at: https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network.
